fields.title: spec: define  marker semantics and injected <skill> format
fields.description:
Decisions for $skill marker + injection (codex-style marker + separate injected instructions message).

## Marker detection
- Syntax: `$` + skill name
- Name charset: `[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*` (lowercase + digits + hyphen).
- Boundary rules:
  - Marker must start at beginning of prompt or be preceded by whitespace.
  - Name ends at the first non `[a-z0-9-]` char (punctuation ok after).
  - Examples matched: `$beads`, `use $beads, please`, `
$beads do X`
  - Examples NOT matched: `$PATH` (uppercase), `foo$beads` (no whitespace boundary), `\$beads` (escaped)

## Escaping
- Escape syntax: `\$name` (a backslash immediately before `$`) disables injection and leaves literal text.

## Multiple skills
- Multiple markers may appear in one prompt.
- Dedupe by skill name, keep the order of first occurrence.

## Prompt text handling
- Do NOT inline-expand the user prompt.
- Keep the `$skill` markers unchanged in the user prompt text (transparent + harmless).

## Injected payload
- Inject a separate extension custom message before the LLM call containing one or more blocks:

```
<skill>
<name>beads</name>
<path>/abs/path/to/SKILL.md</path>
<instructions>
...SKILL.md body (frontmatter stripped)...
</instructions>
</skill>
```

- Injected instructions use pi behavior: strip YAML frontmatter (same as `/skill:name` expansion).

## Size limits
- Apply truncation to injected instructions using pi defaults: max 2000 lines OR 50KB (whichever hits first).
- If truncated, append a note inside `<instructions>`: `[Skill content truncated; full content at: <path>]`.

## Error handling
- Unknown skill name: do not inject; optionally notify user via UI (if available).
- Unreadable SKILL.md: do not inject; notify user via UI (if available).

DoD examples
- `$beads do X` injects beads SKILL.md body as separate message.
- `\$beads` does not inject.
